candidates.stmt: Dear Friends and Neighbours, Soon many of you will be receiving your postal vote ballots for the upcoming local elections.✉️ When casting your vote, I want you to ask yourself one simple question. Do the Tories deserve your vote?🤔 Over the last 13 years, they have failed us in every single conceivable way. They have cut out city’s budget to a shoestring. They have raised our taxes to the highest levels in living memory. They have failed to control the cost of living. They have broken our NHS, and they have failed to control our borders. Despite all this, our city has endured. We have the lowest council tax in the Northeast and our local economy is seeing a boom, with new buildings being erected all over the city.   I have spent the last two years working extremely hard to improve our area, I have reported dozens of potholes and fly-tipping incidents a week and I have volunteered in warm spaces and have seen first-hand the effects of austerity.💪 If elected, I will fight to: 🔴 Keep our council tax the lowest in the Northeast. 🔴 Keep free pest control. 🔴 Keep free bulky waste collections. The conservatives locally are in disarray with one of their own sitting Barnes councillors becoming disillusioned and leaving them.  Another conservative councillor won’t make a difference here in Barnes Ward. 🌹🌹🌹

Barnes is a fantastic area with wonderful residents who deserve to be heard and have someone to fight on their behalf.  I want to be the person residents can rely on to ensure every issue is tackled and Barnes continues to be a wonderful place for residents to live.  Whether it’s a green issue, a back-lane, a pothole or anti-social behaviour I want residents to know they can call on me to fight their corner to ensure Barnes is a clean, green and safe area.  A place people are proud to call home and a place I will be proud to represent.  As a Mam I want to ensure our communities thrive so my boys are proud of Sunderland and the place we call home.

I have lived in Sunderland since 2004. I have been actively involved in a number of organisations within Sunderland including being a Secretary of the Millfield Residents' Association and Chair of the Sunderland LGBTQ group. I have successfully fought for the restoration of the half hourly bus service around The Docks. I am passionate about accessibility and if elected, I will fight for the recognition that elderly people are not necessarily tech savvy and need help and understanding in the field of modern technology.

I will also fight the blockage of pavements to ensure accessibility for people with mobility needs.

Michael has lived in the North East for his whole live, and actively campaigns to improve the area. Michael has campaigned with the Lib Dems across the region to help fight back against the Labour-controlled local authorities that let our communities down.

Working alongside Heather Fagan and Paul Gibson in Doxford Park, Michael has been working to improve the community for residents as part of the local FOCUS team.

Outside of politics, Michael runs a small business. He has previously volunteered with a local Healthwatch, a local charitable organisation helping those suffering from enduring mental health difficulties, served as a trustee for a detatched youth project.

Born in South Shields and raised all over I'm proud to have chosen Sunderland as my home. I've seen this great place do great things with crumbs, and I think we deserve councillors who look to the future. I think Green policies will help Doxford Park make changes that help everyone fairly. I will be your voice on the council and I will work with you, as well as for you.

If elected, I pledge to focus on social justice, including ensuring accessible and affordable public transport in my area, whilst working to protect the environment.

Community is our greatest strength, and we should be able to take great pride in where we live and work. As a resident of Farringdon and Silksworth, you deserve to enjoy a safe and well-maintained local area with diverse green spaces. I have a background in serving the public, having previously worked within the Coroner’s Office and the police, and also as a Trustee for a local charity. If elected, I will use my skills and experience to tackle antisocial behaviour and fly-tipping, and work to make your local area cleaner, greener, and safer.

We’ve all watched as "business as usual" translated into cronyism and backroom deals. You, the taxpayer, have seen enough of the corruption that prioritizes political insiders over public interest. It is time to demand real fairness.
I and The Green Party believe in power that stays where it belongs: in your hands. We are pushing for a future where local services are run by local people, for local people. No more distant mandates or corporate handouts—just a commitment to the neighborhood and city we call home. I'm tired of the same old..., let's change it now - Vote green.

As a Fulwell resident, I know how let down by the council the local community feels. This is a warm, hard-working, diverse community that doesn’t deserve to feel neglected. But Fulwell doesn’t deserve to be taken in by the deception and divisiveness of Reform either. It deserves a party that hears and respects the people, makes sure renters are treated fairly, protects our green spaces and beautiful beaches, our public services and the NHS - one that fosters a community which is clean, accessible and fair for all. This is the Green Party vision for Fulwell.

Paul was born and raised in Sunderland and is a proud Mackem. He attended Southmoor Comprehensive School in Sunderland as well as St Roberts in Washington.

He is currently a Sunderland city councillor for Grindon, Hastings Hill, Springwell and Thorney Close; and is leader of the Liberal Democrat opposition group on the Council.

• I’m a city councillor because I believe in liberal values — standing up for individual freedom, strong local communities, fairness, and giving people a real voice in the decisions that affect their lives.

• I believe politics should be about getting things done, not party games or empty promises. My job is to listen to residents, act on their concerns, and fight their corner in the Civic Centre.

• I live in the ward and stay in regular contact with residents across Grindon, Thorney Close, Hastings Hill, North Farm, and Springwell through my regular FOCUS community newsletter.

• I campaign on the issues that matter most to local people — safer streets, cleaner neighbourhoods, protecting local services, and making sure our communities get the investment and respect they deserve.

• As an opposition councillor, I hold the Council to account, challenge poor decisions, and speak up when residents feel they are not being listened to.

• I believe power should be decentralised, decisions should be made as close to local people as possible, and communities should have more control over their own future.

• If you have a local issue, need support, or simply want to raise a concern, I’m available all year round by phone, email, post, or in person. My door is always open to residents.

At this general election there is a clear choice between Theresa May or Jeremy Corbyn as prime minister to take the country through Brexit and provide the strong leadership needed to keep our economy growing and our public services properly funded.

Brexit must mean Brexit, but the Labour Party is not settled on withdrawal from the EU and the Liberal Democrats are leaving the door open to staying in, endangering our negotiating position and ignoring the will of the people, which must be respected.

None of Sunderland’s Members of Parliament have ever supported Jeremy Corbyn as leader of the Labour Party, but a vote for Labour will put him into Downing Street; a Marxist-inspired economist at the Treasury and Diane Abbott at the Home Office.

The local Labour Party dwells in the past, but says little about the achievements of the city since 2010, during which time investment has topped £1.5billion with major projects such as the New Wear Bridge and the Vaux site now under way.

Under Conservative governments, unemployment in the North East has fallen to a 42-year low and in the Sunderland Central constituency the number of people claiming out-of-work benefits is down by a half, helping local people off benefits and into work.

The UK is the second-fastest growing economy of any advanced nation, which is essential to provide the funding for our schools and hospitals and is put into peril by the high tax and high spending policies of the Labour Party.

Only a Conservative government led by Theresa May will continue to support the economic regeneration of our city; provide strong leadership through Brexit and ensure the economy grows so our schools and hospitals can get the funding they need.

Source: http://www.sunderlandecho.com/news/politics/meet-the-candidates-would-be-mps-bid-for-your-votes-in-sunderland-central-1-8582471

I have become more and more interested in politics over the last decade after a career working for the local authority. I believe the people of Herrington and Newbottle would benefit from Green policies including proper funding into all aspects of people's day to day life.

If elected, I will use my skills and experience for you, making sure that Herrington and Newbottle are connected, supported and respected.

As a director of Houghton le Spring Residents Community Interest Company my interests lie solely in the local Community in which I have lived and worked my whole life.  My passion is the people of Houghton. By working together WITH the residents, I will strive to make Houghton a better/safer place to live, work and raise our families.   Local politics should be about local issues.  I feel Houghton needs to be represented by people who put Houghton first rather than any party they are affiliated to.  My political ethos is transparency, informing and involving the public, something I feel is sadly lacking.  If elected I promise to continue to work for and support Houghton’s residents, traders, and community services.  The only voice YOU have in local politics is YOUR precious vote.   Please use it to shout up for Houghton. VOTE INDEPENDENT and let’s put people before party.

Hi, I’m Steph, your Green Party candidate. I live in Houghton with my husband, kids and our dog, and I’m proud to call this community home. I’m standing as a councillor because I care about where we live and believe residents deserve to be heard. I want to be a voice you can turn to, someone who listens, speaks up, and gets things moving. I’ll work with council services to help get things sorted and keep you informed. From our high street to buses, potholes to anti-social behaviour, these issues matter and I pledge a relentless focus on resolving them!

I have lived in and around the Houghton area all my life and have spent my career working in and around Sunderland. As your councillor, I will take bold action to tackle both the cost-of-living and climate crises and work to establish community energy schemes, bringing cheaper, renewable energy, as well as creating jobs. I will campaign for more affordable housing, the repurposing of empty properties- and brownfield sites, to protect our green spaces. I will also push for Sunderland to be a site for a Universal Basic Income trial– proven to reduce poverty, enable education, entrepreneurship and support carers. Vote for a well overdue change.

I’ve lived in Sunderland most of my life and have raised my family here. I got involved in local politics about three years ago because I want to make a difference, improving the lives of people here. For a long time I’ve been frustrated with the lack of vision for our city. The decline in public services is an issue for many people I speak to, so I would love the opportunity to represent Millfield and Thornholme along with Niall Hodson & Andrew Wood on the local Lib Dem Focus Team, making a real difference for everyone in our community.

I've lived in Pallion & Ford since 2019, and have seen first hand how the council has over looked our area for too long - the people of Pallion deserve better.

Our community deserves functional public transport, affordable, well maintained housing, and a council who listens to our needs - helping us tackle anti social behaviour, fly tipping and criminal damage together.

If elected, I will make sure Pallion & Ford is given the support and attention it deserves, with a focus on what makes this such a great place to live: the people and the community.

I began my professional life as a care worker, before training and working as a nurse. I then worked as a social worker in child protection and later with teenagers and adults with learning disabilities.  I am now a practicing psychotherapist.  Across all of these roles what has remained constant is my commitment to care, dignity and fairness.  Sadly, these professions have given me a front row seat to the ever-increasing ways that people are suffering.

I am passionate about social and environmental justice.  I believe there is more than enough in this world for everyone to live well if resources were shared more fairly.  Yet under the current system, where power and wealth are hoarded by the few, everyday people are struggling more and more.  It’s wrong and it needs to stop.

For me, social and environmental justice cannot be separated. The way we treat each other and the way we treat the planet are deeply connected. A fairer society and a healthier environment go hand in hand.

I live in Pennywell, and, like most people, I worry about the rising cost-of-living, particularly the rise in energy prices. As a councillor, I would push for a comprehensive home insulation scheme – the cheapest energy bill, with the lowest carbon emissions, is one you don’t have to pay because your home is so well-insulated. I’m not a career politician, I’m an ordinary working man and I believe more needs to be done to support ordinary people. That includes investing in an affordable, well-integrated public transport system to allow us to go about our lives. Local bus services should be in public hands so councils can set routes and timetables, making sure services run where they’re needed, not where they make most money. The Green Party believes in people led politics, if you vote for me, I will listen and take my lead from you, the residents of Pennywell and South Hylton.

I have lived in the area my whole life and have seen the complacency of the Labour Party with it's near total control for years and years. I will do my utmost best to protect our green spaces and countryside. Work with local agencies, including the police to combat anti-social behaviour. I pledge to work hard to help reopen the Leamside Rail Line, which will bring opportunities and better travel for local residents. The Green Party offers hope and fresh new ideas. A different voice to the tired two of Labour and the Conservatives, and the hate of Reform.

I was born in Sunderland and have lived around the city all my life. I am now currently residing in Thornhill, Sunderland. My family background is in shipbuilding, steelwork, and heavy industry. I have had a long career in financial services, and I am proud of the history of our city but wish for a safer, prosperous, and prouder future.

Like many residents, the city holds a special place in my heart. I am not a career politician parachuted into Sunderland from hundreds of miles away, but I care deeply about the direction our city is heading in. Sunderland’s best days could well be ahead of us if we are willing to push for a brighter future. Reform UK is the only show in town to deliver this bright future!

My main political points of concern facing Sunderland are:

- Lifting the tax threshold to £20k gives Sunderland workers an extra £1,500 a year in their pocket, thus helping reduce the 5.5 million in out-of-work benefits by making work pay.

- Campaigning for net zero immigration to stop wage compression and secure our borders by a zero-tolerance approach to illegal boat crossings.

-Attracting high-quality and well-paid employment to Sunderland by backing the return of maritime industries via the existing government national shipbuilding strategy, along with scrapping business rates to encourage small businesses to thrive.

-Cheaper energy and zero NHS waiting lists via our fully costed plan at: https://www.reformparty.uk/reformisessential

Vote for change this election, vote to make Britain great, and vote Reform UK for Sunderland Central.

I am an award-wining Sunderland-based artist and space science educator, and firmly believe in creating a real future for Sunderland. The North East is experiencing a new industrial revolution in space science, technology and the creative industries. As a specialist in these fields, I promote the use of space technology to improve the environment and to invent new methods of producing energy. As a professional artist, I work with thousands of young people every year to create positive, inclusive, fair opportunities for their careers. I exhibit regionally, nationally and internationally, promoting Sunderland’s best features. Vote for our future.

I have lived in the Southwick ward for most of life and I believe Green policies would benefit the area. In the past 3 years I was part of the team that setup the local parkrun in Fulwell Quarry, where I volunteer most weeks and since then I have gotten to know many people within the local area who have started coming along since we started. I am also on the committee of Thompson Park Community Centre, where I regularly volunteer in various roles.

If elected, I will continue to work for the people of Southwick.

Hi, I'm Richard. I have campaigned against council decisions for years, and I want to change things from the inside this time around. I believe the council should always put residents first, listen to people and include them in decision making. I pledge to be your green voice in the council chamber.

I’ve lived in Sunderland my entire life. I graduated from Sunderland University, taught in schools across the city, worked in Sunderland Royal Hospital and now run my own training company opposite the Crown Works development. I’m a passionate advocate for this city and the communities that support it. I don’t believe it is a place defined by division and hatred, but I do think our voices go ignored too often by our elected leaders and I have joined politics and the Green Party to give a powerful local voice to my community.

Hi, I’m Dale. I have lived in Tunstall and Humbledon for the past 5 years, and I am passionate about making the ward an even better place to live, work and unwind. My aims for the ward are better community cohesion, cleaner and safer streets and parks, and ensuring local services are run as efficiently as possible.

I am proud to have served as a councillor for Washington central Ward since 2008, as an experienced councillor in standing as a candidate again as I believe I have the experience and skills needed to support my community during these difficult times.

Please visit my Facebook page to find out more about me.

I’ve worked in the police, industry, mental health nursing, and now as a counselling psychotherapist specialising in neurodivergence.

As an autistic man, social justice is something I’ve lived, not just believed in. I’ve seen first-hand the gaps in mental health services and the negative impact on support systems.

I’ve supported hundreds of people across the North East, helping them through crisis and toward better mental health, in clinic and schools.

I also led a successful community campaign against unfair heating systems, which we won, and set up a Community Interest Company providing free support to autistic people and their families.

For the last 15 years, I have worked with the local community through a number of projects focusing on health and wellbeing, employability and culture. I know Washington would benefit from Green policies such as proper funding for accessible and sustainable public transport and better waste management. Residents' views on these issues are too often ignored by the current Labour council- this is why we need a local Washington Town Council to bring local government closer to the local people

If elected, I will use my skills and experience for you, ensuring that Washington East is connected, supported and respected.

I believe in prioritising the needs and concerns of the community above all else, focusing on people before politics. I am dedicated to creating positive change and serving the people of Washington, the North-East and ultimately our country. I want to shake up the political landscape both locally and nationally.

I am not your ‘typical career politician’ that`s for sure, and that is what we need in the Northeast, someone who will put people before politics.

I got involved with politics in 2019 because I wanted to improve the lives of those around me, so I joined the Conservative Party because traditional British values are important to me and I was lead to believe that this was the home for patriots; I was later elected as a Councillor for Washington in 2021.

Democracy, the rule of law, individual liberty, and mutual respect are all things that matter to me, and I had expected to see this within the party, however the Conservative Party are no longer conservative, they no longer represent the values I, and many others believe in, and so found a home in Reform UK, defecting in 2023.

I could see that our flawed political system was a magnet for those seeking power, rather than serving the public, too few politicians are focusing on the public's interests. There is a real lack of quality Councillors and MPs, many serve their own interest and those of the party they serve, with the public being an afterthought. Like many people I believe we need to have strong politicians with leadership skills and the ability to inspire and unite communities.

I want to address these underlying issues by promoting transparency, accountability, and inclusivity in politics and help attract and retaining competent individuals as Councillors and MPs.

As your next MP, I will be committed to transparency, integrity, and accountability in politics. Reform UK have NO whip system which means I am free to vote and work in your best interest, not that of any political party or union.

I began

Born and bred in Washington I have raised my two daughters here, I have strong ties within the community and understand the growing concerns among residents.

Having witnessed first-hand the real effect of years of austerity, I have become increasingly aware of the indifference from both the conservative & Labour Party towards the hard-working people of the North East, as a former Labour Party member I strongly believe like many others, labour has deserted the working-class voters who built the party, it is time for change and reform within our society which is why I chose to join Reform UK.

I am passionate about making a difference within our area and striving towards becoming a stronger, better-represented Washington.

I am incredibly proud to be selected to stand up for my ward in Washington North.

Commenting on the selection Cllr Paul Donaghy, Washington & Sunderland west spokesman said

" Elizabeth knows Washington North very well, She understands what needs to be done and how to best help the residents across the ward. Elizabeth is part of the Reform team who will be working to put Washington first"  To keep up to date with Elizabeth`s campaign visit  www.facebook.com/reformwaswnorth

My pledges to you
If elected, my number 1 and overriding priority is to do my best for the people in Washington North Ward.
I will strive at all times to work hard and fight for Washington’s fair share from our city.
I want to foster and bring back a sense of community.  I want to create more awareness of things to do locally, both new and established. Youth clubs, groups etc. Helping to create a sense of pride and community in where we live whilst reducing anti-social behaviour
 Support residents and be a mouthpiece to highlight issues. Assist in getting them resolved in a timely manner.
 Always respond to queries in an honest and timely manner. I might not know the answer or be able to get a response that pleases everyone but I pledge to answer honestly with reasoning and if i don’t know – find someone who does! I want to be open, visible and approachable

I live locally and am fed up with Sunderland Council. Every councillor in Washington
is from the Labour party. It’s not healthy for local democracy and it shows. They say
there’s no money for Washington, but they’re spending millions on new council
offices in Sunderland. I want to stand up to years of failure, neglect and complacency
from Labour’s council bosses in Sunderland and fight to improve our communities.
Please lend me and the Liberal Democrats your vote in this year’s council election.
Washington deserves better than just another Labour councillor who is more
interested in Sunderland than our own town.

A proud Washington resident for over 50 years, Robert is a married, retired time-served tradesman who helped build the original Washington New Town back in the day. Starting on the tools, he worked his way up to Clerk of Works for the Regional Health Authority before setting up his own successful company. He went on to deliver major projects for health authorities, Durham University and local councils across the country and internationally, working in Northern and Southern Europe, Africa and South America – but always taking the same practical, no-nonsense approach.

With strong experience in highways and road construction, Robert knows only too well the difference between proper reinstatement and the repeated pothole patching that continues to frustrate so many residents. Now retired and living in Fatfield, he is standing for Reform UK in Washington South because it’s time for straight-talking, common-sense representation that actually puts local people first.

Robert is determined to make a real difference right where he lives in Fatfield and right across the South ward communities of Rickleton, Harraton, Staithes, Teal Farm, Mount Pleasant, and the Wear & Pattinson areas.

Robert’s Priorities:

- Fixing our roads properly - Drawing on his highways expertise, Robert will demand genuine, lasting reinstatement works so we stop wasting money on the same potholes year after year.  
- Standing up for Fatfield and local services - Making sure Fatfield and all our estates get the attention they deserve on roads, verges, drainage, bin collections and cleaner streets.  
- Better value for council tax - Fighting for reliable services and sensible spending that delivers real results for residents and businesses across Washington South.  
- Sensible development - Supporting community-led improvements that genuinely benefit local people, such as better sports and recreational facilities with adequate parking, while listening carefully to residents and protecting our valued gre

Elouise is a passionate health professional and dedicated towards championing local issues. She has lived in Washington with her family for many years. Her long career working right across Wearside has given her extensive hands-on experience delivering public services, so she really understands the everyday challenges that families here face. She is determined to fight for real change and make sure Washington, and especially our ward, gets the investment and attention it deserves.

For years, Elouise has supported children, young people and their families across Washington and Wearside. She has made a real difference to their lives. She has also represented the North East at national level, helping to shape policies that actually improve things on the ground.

She is standing because she has had enough of Washington being treated as second-best to the city centre. For far too long our ward has been overlooked on investment, local services and having our voices properly heard. While millions have been poured into the city centre, our families and young people have been left behind – whether it’s decent community facilities, safe places to play, or simply being listened to. That stops now.

Elouise is warm, energetic and fiercely determined. She brings fresh energy, real-life experience and a direct approach. She is committed to standing up for residents who feel ignored or let down.

Elouise focuses on:
- Fair funding and proper investment right across the ward: in Rickleton, Harraton, Fatfield, Staithes, Teal Farm, and Mount Pleasant; along with our industrial areas of Wear and Pattinson
- Better facilities and community spaces for families, children and young people
- High-quality local services that actually deliver
- Strong support for local clubs and community venues
- Ensuring residents’ voices are heard loud and clear

Her aim is simple: she will listen to residents, stand up for what really matters, and do her utmost to get things done. No more excuses, no mo

John is a longstanding Harraton resident. Fed up with years of the same old Labour control that’s let down our communities, John’s throwing his hat in the ring to fight for real change - putting Washington first and making sure our voices are actually heard at the council table.

John knows what matters to families and workers around here. He’s seen first-hand how decisions made in the council chamber affect everyday life in Washington – from potholed roads and creaking services to housing pressures and the cost of living squeezing people. Right at the top of his list is holding big housing providers like Gentoo to account so that local tenants actually get the decent homes they pay for.

John’s key focuses:

Holding Gentoo to account - Cracking down on the damp, mould and repair delays that are blighting homes across Washington, pushing for faster action on anti-social behaviour, and making sure tenants’ rent money goes on fixing properties – not high executive salaries or pet projects elsewhere. Enough is enough! Washington deserves better from its biggest landlord.

Fixing our streets and services - Proper maintenance of roads, pavements and parks so Washington looks and feels like a place we can be proud of again, not an afterthought.

Standing up for Washington’s identity - Protecting our green spaces, opposing over-development that ignores local needs, and ensuring new housing comes with the infrastructure to support it.

Cutting waste and bureaucracy - Ending the endless spending on consultants and pet projects while front-line services struggle.

Safer communities - More visible action on anti-social behaviour so people feel secure in their own villages.

John believes Sunderland City Council has been run in the interests of the few for too long. With Reform UK, he wants to bring straight-talking, accountable representation that delivers for the many - ending the grip of the old parties, getting tough with under-performing landlords like Gentoo, and finally

Hello, I am Fiona Miller. I have been an elected member since 2011. During this time, I have had the honour to work for you and support local projects and residents’ associations. A firm believer in supporting my local community and listening to what you want done in the ward. Working with you and for you. I pledge to be your voice on the council and ensure your voice is heard in the council chamber. I will fight for decent and affordable housing and good transport links in my ward.

Hi, I’m Theo, one of your Green Party candidates for Washington South. I live in Washington and I am part of this wonderful community, but I feel how let down Washington has been by the council and the Government. I’m passionate about changing this and being a voice for the people, standing up for the vulnerable in our community and working together to build a safer, happier place to live.

Hello, I'm Peter and I'm the Green candidate for Washington South. I live in the Teal Farm and I am proud to call Washington my home. From Nissan Factory to the Galleries and beyond, I love being part of this great community. I feel that only the green party can represent Washington properly and is the only party that has local matters at heart. After serving in the merchant navy for over a decade, I now work in naval regulation and development. With these skills and knowledge, and your support, I will be your effective voice at the council.

I am standing for re-election to Sunderland Council on May 7th 2026 for the new Washington West ward made up from the villages of Ayton, Blackfell, Oxclose & Springwell Village.

I live in Ayton, ensuring a local representative can be voted into Washington.

Here are my pledges for voters in Washington West

FIXING OUR ROADS
- £1M extra funding secured to resurface our roads.

MORE LITTER AND GRIT BINS
- We will fund areas which need more litter and grit bins.

BETTER PLAY SPACES
- Investing in local play equipment across parks.

FUNDING YOUTH PROVISION
- £500k more funding for our youth provision.

CCTV IMPROVEMENTS
- To tackle flytipping, littering, and anti-social behaviour.

WORK ALL YEAR ROUND AS YOUR COUNCILLORS
- We will be visible and approachable in the community.

Born and raised in the ward Joanne is a passionate advocate for residents, Well known for her years of volunteering in the area and her warm and friendly nature. On being asked why she was running she simply stated "I'm not the most political person but I've reached the limit to what I can achieve as a resident, By running I'm hoping I can go further and help more people " After meeting her while she was out on her campaign I found her an absolute breath of fresh air, A very genuine young lady who comes across as refreshingly honest with a keen sense of humour.

I’ve lived in Springwell Village for 20 years. I’m an RAF veteran, former vocational training manager for a Sunderland charity, and now work as a Quality Consultant for a vocational qualification awarding body.
For too long, consecutive Labour councils in Sunderland have taken Washington and Springwell Village for granted. After more than half a century of control, they’ve poured money into the city centre and seafront while hitting us with above-inflation council tax rises, yet delivered little in the way of services for Springwell Village, Blackfell, Ayton and Oxclose.
We deserve far better!
That’s why I’m standing. If you elect me and the other Reform UK candidates as your councillors on 7th May, you will never be ignored again. We will always be there, standing up for Springwell Village and the whole Washington West ward – fighting for what matters most to you.
This election gives us a real chance to send a clear message to Keir Starmer and Westminster, and to change local politics for good. It’s a straight fight between Reform UK and Labour.
A Reform-led council would bring exciting plans for the area, including better youth services and improved parks. I will put local residents first and ensure Washington West is never overlooked again.
My priorities include:
• Improving road safety - fixing potholes and tackling dangerous and reckless driving
• Protecting our green belt, parks and heritage
• Improving social cohesion by cracking down on crime and anti-social behaviour
Don’t waste this opportunity. Use all three of your votes for Reform UK candidates in Washington West on 7th May and help change local politics for good!

I've lived in Washington for many years and see the challenges our community faces every day. I grew tired of the empty promises from politicians who talk a good game but deliver little on the ground long before I retired from policing in 2025. After retiring, I was approached by Reform UK to help the Washington & Gateshead South branch push for real, practical change right here in our communities.
On the doorsteps across Washington residents keep raising the same concerns: roads that aren't maintained properly or safe enough, anti-social behaviour that makes our neighbourhoods and villages feel neglected, and a lack of visible policing that actually prevents trouble before it starts. I’ve seen first-hand from my time in the force how prevention and proper community presence make all the difference, and I’m determined to make sure those voices are heard loud and clear.
I focus on:
• Better maintained and safer roads with proper enforcement
• Proper crackdowns on anti-social behaviour (ASB) so families can feel secure again
• Support for more effective community policing with stronger prevention measures to improve neighbourhood safety
Your local Original Action Team is about putting local people first, and I’m proud to be part of the team making that happen on the ground in Washington. With three votes available in this election, please use all three to back Reform UK and get the real change Washington deserves.

I worked in adult mental health for over 26 years and spent a decade as a union rep. Like most people I used to find myself following the herd and backing Labour, but I’ve seen too many families in our communities too scared to leave their homes and too many children getting hurt on our streets. As a foster carer I couldn’t stand by any longer, so I joined Reform UK to protect our children and families from unnecessary harm and to fight for the safer neighbourhoods we all deserve.
Whilst I live just over the county boundary, I work across Washington and Sunderland. With Lee and Adrian I’m proud to be covering Washington West, and we’re already rolling up our sleeves - reporting road and infrastructure repair issues, chasing up grit bins that have been missing for eight years, and talking to residents about the real difference Reform will make.
My priorities are:
• Tackling anti-social behaviour by working with residents, the police and the council to reclaim our streets and make Washington West safer for everyone
• Improving provision for children and young people with better local facilities, activities and support so our kids and teenagers have safe, positive places to go

The politics of Labour and Reform are extremely similar. They don't see the value of investment into public services. They will keep cutting and cutting what you rely on and value until there's nothing left. The Greens are the only party to say no to this. Austerity hasn't worked globally, it hasn't worked nationally and it doesn't work for Washington West.

I moved to Washington a few years ago to join my partner and family in their hometown, and they have passed on their love for Washington to me absolutely. Washington West is a vibrant area with a fantastic community and so many beautiful green spaces - it's just been allowed to be forgotten by Sunderland Council. The community here is so strong and has welcomed me with open arms, and I will use my voice in the council to make sure that your voices cannot be ignored any longer.

I am a family man with three children and have lived in Sunderland or Washington all my life. I work in the renewable energy industry as an offshore engineer. Renewable energy is very close to my heart. I believe it can help us tackle climate breakdown and pollution on an international level while providing good quality employment. I also believe it is part of the answer to the cost-of-living crisis. As a councillor, I would campaign for the council to stop investing in fossil fuels and instead invest in community owned renewable energy sources. These could provide cheaper energy to residents and businesses and attract new investment to the area. More money in people’s pockets would provide a boost to the local economy. It would give me great pride to represent our community and be involved in such positive changes. Vote Green for a brighter, more sustainable future.
